message:
fix(ffs-core): free old physical extent when overwriting btrfs regular extents

When the btrfs write path overwrites an existing regular extent at the
same file offset, the old physical extent allocation was leaked: the new
extent was inserted into the fs_tree but the old disk_bytenr/num_bytes
region was never returned to the extent allocator. Over time this causes
the filesystem to run out of space despite data being overwritten rather
than appended.

The fix checks for an existing regular extent at the target offset
before inserting the new one. If found (and disk_bytenr > 0, ruling out
sparse holes), the old physical extent is freed via
extent_alloc.free_extent(). Failures to free are logged as warnings
rather than propagated, since the write itself should still succeed --
the space leak is preferable to a write error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
